utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey charles i'm using this cool new recording device to help me with my documentation is that okay with you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: awesome how are you doing today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i could be better you know i moved out in the city

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: about two years ago bought this big plot of land

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: oh

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i love it lots of deer round awesome

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you be hunter

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: huge hunter i love hunter yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have a refrigerator full of venison at home

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you want to carry share sure yeah we can switch

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice but so the thing is i have noticed that for some reason my breathing

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is n't what it used to be

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah it's you know i i seem to catch my breath a lot more than i used to and i consider myself to be pretty healthy

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you look pretty healthy

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: thanks so do you thank you you you're feeling short of breath

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay is there you know is there any other symptoms that you have with that i get like you know mid shortness of breath suddenly for some reason i'm not quite sure why

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: eyes they're water does n't make very much sense to me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i always think it's allergies

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mostly could be

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have a dog that eyes water like that she always gets eyebugers

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i get eyebugers too

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: crazy uh the last time it happened i went to the urgent care and they gave me a nebulizer that helped

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they prescribed me an inhaler that i use now when i feel the symptoms coming on

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay great do you ever notice a rash with any of this

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you have any nausea vomiting

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: besides when you go out heavy drinking right

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean i diarrhea quite a bit once i go out

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i understand that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: heavy drinking

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah do you ever get lip or throat swelling

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you ever had any issues with allergies in the past

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmmm what situations do you notice the symptoms come on when is it when you're near your cat or outside the house

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have noticed them in all three situations

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: in the house when the cat is n't around and outside as well

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmmm yeah i'm not a big cat person

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no neither

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you do you have any symptoms now well which is

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no no travels and no symptoms right now

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright so i'm gon na do a physical exam for you right now your vital signs look good your pulse is okay and yeah your pulse ox is normal so that's good you do n't appear in any distress you may be might be a little bit nervous to come in and see me but looks pretty good i do not appreciate any rash on your body there is no angioedema which is just swelling of your lips like you mentioned no audible stridor which is a bad noise in your airway when it gets swollen so that's good news if you just want to take a deep breath listening to your lungs on your lungs exam i do appreciate some faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields so i know you had a chest x-ray when you came in i'm looking at that chest x-ray right now and your pulmonary function test and they were both normal so let's talk a little bit about what i think is going on for your first problem you have newly diagnosed allergic asthma so i want you to continue the albuterol inhaler i do n't want you to wait until your symptoms flare up or are bad take it as soon as you start to feel any symptoms at all i'm gon na prescribe something else called singulair ten milligrams you might have seen some commercials for it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you take that once daily and that's gon na help decrease the occurrences of your asthma

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i also am gon na proceed with allergy testing have you ever had an allergy test before

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay we'll start with skin testing and we'll see if we can target what the triggers in hopes in hopes that we can avoid any any other management and this can be successful if we ca n't figure out what it is from that we'll have to discuss more testing in your blood and do immunotherapy so i wan na see you next week to schedule the skin testing do you have any questions for me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: should i come my cats

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes

detail_list.summary: Charles Sullivan is a 45-year-old male presenting with shortness of breath.
The patient enjoys hunting.
Shortness of breath.
The patient reports shortness of breath that began two years ago.
The patient reports sudden episodes of shortness of breath without a clear cause.
The patient reports epiphora with rheum.
The patient received a nebulizer treatment at urgent care for his symptoms.
The patient uses an inhaler when symptoms occur.
The doctor asks if the patient has a rash.
The patient denies having a rash.
The doctor asks if the patient has nausea or vomiting.
The patient denies nausea and vomiting.
The patient reports diarrhea after heavy alcohol consumption.
The doctor asks if the patient has lip or throat swelling.
The patient denies lip or throat swelling.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of allergies.
The patient denies previous issues with allergies.
The doctor asks about triggers for the patient's symptoms.
The patient reports symptoms occur in the house, near the cat, and outside.
The doctor asks if the patient is currently experiencing symptoms.
The patient denies current symptoms.
Physical exam findings include normal oxygen saturation, no distress, no rash, no angioedema, no stridor, and faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally.
Chest x-ray and pulmonary function tests are normal.
The patient is diagnosed with allergic asthma.
The patient is instructed to use the albuterol inhaler at the onset of symptoms and is prescribed Singulair 10 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient has had an allergy test before.
The patient denies having had an allergy test.
The doctor plans for allergy skin testing in one week.
The patient asks if he should avoid his cats.
The doctor confirms the patient should avoid his cats.
